arts.ttl: Tuttosport: Ultras leader’s alleged communication with Inter legend Materazzi revealed
arts.sum: A new development has emerged in the ongoing investigation into the ultras groups of Inter Milan and AC Milan. According to reports from Tuttosport, Marco Ferdico, a prominent Inter ultras leader who was recently arrested, had a phone conversation with former Inter defender Marco Materazzi regarding police activities. The investigation, which has led to 19 arrests and effectively dismantled the leadership of both Milan clubs’ ultras groups, continues to uncover complex relationships between the fan organizations and club officials. Massimiliano Silva, an Inter executive responsible for fan relations, has provided a statement to investigators. arts.ttl: Inter eyeing defender Jonathan Tah on free transfer
arts.sum: Bayer Leverkusen defender Jonathan Tah has become a hot topic in the transfer market as his contract nears its end. The 27-year-old German international has confirmed he won’t be renewing with the Bundesliga club, sparking interest from several top European teams. Calciomercato report that Inter Milan has been closely monitoring Tah’s situation for some time. The Nerazzurri are looking to bolster their defensive options, potentially as replacements for aging center-backs Francesco Acerbi and Stefan de Vrij. Tah’s potential availability on a free transfer makes him an attractive prospect for Inter, who are always on the lookout for cost-effective signings. arts.ttl: Wiretap reveals Ultras leader’s ticket request to Inter coach Inzaghi
arts.sum: A recent investigation into Italian football ultras has uncovered a revealing phone conversation between Inter Milan’s head coach Simone Inzaghi and Marco Ferdico, a prominent ultras leader who was recently arrested. La Gazzetta dello Sport report that the discussion, which took place in May 2023, centered around obtaining additional tickets for the Champions League final in Istanbul. According to the intercepted call, Ferdico explicitly asked Inzaghi to intervene with the club’s management, specifically mentioning CEO Beppe Marotta, to secure 200 extra tickets for the ultras group. Inzaghi reportedly promised to intercede with the club’s top executives on their behalf. arts.ttl: Sportmediaset: Inter and Milan face potential administrative oversight, players at risk of suspension
arts.sum: In the wake of a recent investigation that led to the arrest of 19 ultras, both Inter and AC Milan find themselves in a precarious situation. The Italian Football Federation (FIGC) has requested the case files, raising questions about potential consequences for the clubs and their registered members. Club Consequences Sportmediaset (via Calciomercato) report that for Inter Milan, a “preventive procedure” has been initiated by the prosecutor’s office. Consultants have been appointed to assess whether the club has severed ties with the ultras. If the club fails to demonstrate this, it could face administrative oversight. arts.sum: Reports from Italy suggest Inter Ultras had a meeting with the clubs former defender, Milan Skriniar. According to the report from Calciomercato, the influence and pressure exerted by Inter’s organized supporters and their top representatives seemingly reached everywhere, even inside the locker room of Simone Inzaghi’s team. arts.ttl: Champions League | Inter v Red Star: Probable starting lineups
arts.sum: Red Star Belgrade, the second opponent for Simone Inzaghi’s Inter in this Champions League campaign, are preparing to face the Italian giants with a degree of confidence, having won five of their last six matches. Manager Vladan Milojevic is expected to stick with his preferred 4-2-3-1 formation for this crucial encounter. In goal, Ilic is likely to get the nod over Glazer. The defense is set to feature Seol and Rodic as fullbacks, with Dkusic and Dijga forming the central defensive partnership. The midfield will likely see former AC Milan player Rade Krunic partnering with Elsnik. arts.ttl: Inter vs Red Star Belgrade: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: After kicking off their Champions League campaign with an impressive draw at Manchester City, Inter will be looking to secure their first win of the league phase when they face Red Star Belgrade on Tuesday night. Red Star Belgrade, meanwhile, were beaten at home by Benfica to start their league phase campaign.

arts.ttl: Champions League predictions: Tuesday's matchday 2 fixtures
arts.sum: Inter, who made a huge statement on matchday one by stifling Man City at the Etihad Stadium, will expect to kick on and consolidate with Red Star Belgrade at home. Looking to get off the mark for the season, Manchester City travel to Slovakia to face Slovan Bratislava, while another heavyweight in Barcelona are home to Young Boys of Bern.
